title: Closed-Loop Feedback Control System
subject: Control Systems (CS)
textbook_reference: Benjamin C. Kuo, Automatic Control Systems, Chapter 1, Page 8

recent_questions: Explain closed-loop control system with a block diagram. State its advantages over open-loop system (Dec 2023).
Draw block diagram of negative feedback system and find its transfer function C(s)/R(s) (May 2023).

theory: A closed-loop control system (feedback control system) is a system that maintains a prescribed relationship between the output and the reference input by comparing them and using the difference (error) as a means of control. It consists of: (1) Reference Input (R(s)) - the desired value; (2) Comparator/Summing Point - compares input and output; (3) Error Signal (E(s)) - the difference; (4) Controller/Plant (G(s)) - acts on error to produce output; (5) Output (C(s)) - the controlled variable; (6) Feedback Path (H(s)) - measures output and feeds it back to the summing point. Negative feedback reduces system sensitivity to parameter variations and disturbances.
key_points: Summing point calculates error signal: E(s) = R(s) - B(s).
Forward path transfer function: G(s) = C(s) / E(s).
Feedback path transfer function: H(s) = B(s) / C(s).
Closed-loop transfer function: T(s) = G(s) / (1 + G(s)H(s)) for negative feedback.
Improves system bandwidth, stability, and reduces steady-state error.
use_cases: Automobile cruise control and aerospace autopilot guidance systems.
